response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced polyglot engineer with a strong craftsmanship ethos, but his background is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His primary technical identity is TypeScript/JavaScript/Node.js and DLang — Java appears only in peripheral mentions (robotic arms, mobile orchestration tooling), and Kotlin is entirely absent from his resume. There is no evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java framework experience, and no demonstrated knowledge of JVM internals. His only IDE-adjacent work (NI LabWindows/CVI) involved C/C++ components, not JVM tooling. The role demands deep JVM ecosystem expertise as a baseline requirement, which this candidate does not meet.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship culture: consistent application of TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development across roles
Developer tooling experience: built test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), MCP code generators, and contributed to IDE-adjacent systems at National Instruments
Experience with large-scale, high-throughput systems (Sociomantic: millions of HTTP requests/second), demonstrating engineering maturity
concerns: Primary technical stack is TypeScript/JavaScript/Node.js and DLang — Java is listed in skills but only appears in peripheral, non-primary roles
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java/Kotlin framework experience
No demonstrated JVM internals knowledge, enterprise IDE tooling experience, or Kotlin proficiency — all core requirements of the role

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise Java framework experience
